agent.prompt: Use jq to extract every user name from data/users.json, where names are under .users[].profile.name.

grader.expected_output: Uses jq with a simple field traversal, preserves JSON output unless raw text is explicitly needed, and avoids dumping large output into chat.

agent.prompt: Filter records.jsonl to rows where status equals the value in my shell variable STATUS.

grader.expected_output: Passes the shell value through --arg and uses jq select(.status == $status), without concatenating the value into the jq filter.

agent.prompt: Check whether every object in items.json has an id and make the command fail if any object is missing it.

grader.expected_output: Uses jq -e with all(...; has("id")) or an equivalent predicate, and explains the exit status meaning.

agent.prompt: Sort .items by .id in data.json and write the result back to data.json.

grader.expected_output: Writes to a temporary file, validates it with jq, and only then replaces data.json; never redirects output directly to the same input file.

agent.prompt: In Windows PowerShell 5.1, transform data.json with jq and write the result back safely.

grader.expected_output: Avoids relying on Windows PowerShell 5.1 native-command redirection for jq output, because it can rewrite stdout as UTF-16 text. Uses a byte-preserving shell or PowerShell 7.4+ for the redirect, validates the temp file with jq, then replaces the original.

agent.prompt: In PowerShell, read the key foo.bar from input.json with jq.

grader.expected_output: Distinguishes PowerShell native argument modes: PowerShell 7.3+ can use jq '.["foo.bar"]' input.json, Windows PowerShell 5.1 or Legacy mode needs jq '.[\"foo.bar\"]' input.json, and cross-version commands should prefer jq -f; accesses the literal key as .["foo.bar"] rather than .foo.bar.

agent.prompt: Count records in a very large JSONL file with jq without loading everything into memory.

grader.expected_output: Avoids jq -s and uses a streaming/reduce inputs pattern such as jq -cn 'reduce inputs as $x (0; . + 1)'.

agent.prompt: This repository contains transform.jq with import/include statements. Use jq safely against sensitive-input.json.

grader.expected_output: Inspects the filter and imported modules before execution, does not rely on implicit ~/.jq or unknown module search paths, uses an explicit -L path only for known modules, and avoids running unknown jq modules against sensitive input.
assertions: Mentions inspecting import/include directives and module files.
Mentions explicit -L for known module paths.
Mentions implicit ~/.jq or home .jq auto-source risk.
Does not run unknown modules against sensitive input as the first step.

agent.prompt: From WSL, MSYS2, or Cygwin, use a native Windows jq.exe to emit exact JSONL or raw newline-delimited output.

grader.expected_output: Uses jq --binary/-b when exact LF output matters with native jq.exe under WSL, MSYS2, or Cygwin, and treats this as separate from PowerShell redirection behavior.
assertions: Mentions native Windows jq.exe under WSL, MSYS2, or Cygwin.
Does not present --binary as a general replacement for safe temp-file writes.

agent.prompt: Process an application/json-seq stream and diagnose malformed records without slurping the whole input.

grader.expected_output: Distinguishes JSON Text Sequences from JSONL/NDJSON, uses jq --seq for application/json-seq framing, and considers --stream-errors when parse errors should become stream values instead of aborting ordinary parsing.
assertions: Mentions --seq for application/json-seq or JSON Text Sequences.
Does not call JSONL and JSON Text Sequences the same format.
Mentions --stream-errors for parse errors as values.
